changelog: ### v1.0.8b2 - Orbit rate returns cleanly to a stop

- The orbit rate now comes to a complete, reliable stop. Once you had moved the orbit speed away from zero, it was hard — sometimes impossible — to bring it back to a full standstill: the rate would slip from a faint drift one way straight past zero to a faint drift the other way, so the satellites never quite came to rest. Turning the encoder back down now always lands exactly on a complete stop, and spinning it quickly still ramps smoothly through the range.
### v1.0.8 - Steadier TIME CV, reliable saving & startup version display
- The startup screen now shows both firmware versions — the display/UI version and the audio engine (DSP) version — so you can confirm both halves of Eisei are up to date at a glance.

- TIME CV now works as a true position control. Patching a sawtooth LFO, envelope, or any moving signal into TIME CV used to slowly drag the "zero" point counterclockwise, so your orbits would creep out of place over time. TIME CV is now read as an absolute position: 0V holds at the start, 2.5V is half a lap around, 5V is a full lap back to the start, and negative voltages move the other way. No more drift.
- Steady playheads at rest. With nothing patched (or a resting signal) into TIME CV, electrical noise no longer makes the playheads jitter — they now sit perfectly still.

- SAT long-press now returns the orbit cleanly to zero. Long-pressing SAT to reset orbit position used to leave a moving, locked orbit frozen with its satellites fanned out near — but not at — the start. It now snaps exactly back to zero.
- No more duplicate entries in the datum list. The list was showing the 8 built-in presets and your saved recordings twice over; each datum now appears only once.

- Assigning a datum to a slot no longer asks you to save. Holding SET after assigning a datum to a slot used to pop the save prompt even when you hadn't recorded anything new. Assigning a slot now just stores it quietly.

- Saving no longer gets stuck. Every so often, confirming a save would leave the screen frozen on "Saving…" and the only way out was to power-cycle the module. Saving is now reliable across repeated saves, and on the rare chance one doesn't go through, the screen clears itself after a few seconds so you can simply try again instead of rebooting.
### v1.0.8b1 - TIME CV fixes & startup version display
- The startup screen now shows both firmware versions — the display/UI version and the audio engine (DSP) version — so you can confirm both halves of Eisei are up to date at a glance.

- TIME CV now works as a true position control. Patching a sawtooth LFO, envelope, or any moving signal into TIME CV used to slowly drag the "zero" point counterclockwise, so your orbits would creep out of place over time. TIME CV is now read as an absolute position: 0V holds at the start, 2.5V is half a lap around, 5V is a full lap back to the start, and negative voltages move the other way. No more drift.
- Steady playheads at rest. With nothing patched (or a resting signal) into TIME CV, electrical noise no longer makes the playheads jitter — they now sit perfectly still.

- SAT long-press now returns the orbit cleanly to zero. Long-pressing SAT to reset orbit position used to leave a moving, locked orbit frozen with its satellites fanned out near — but not at — the start. It now snaps exactly back to zero.
- No more duplicate entries in the datum list. The list was showing the 8 built-in presets and your saved recordings twice over; each datum now appears only once.

- Assigning a datum to a slot no longer asks you to save. Holding SET after assigning a datum to a slot used to pop the save prompt even when you hadn't recorded anything new. Assigning a slot now just stores it quietly.

- Saving no longer gets stuck. Every so often, confirming a save would leave the screen frozen on "Saving…" and the only way out was to power-cycle the module. Saving is now reliable across repeated saves, and on the rare chance one doesn't go through, the screen clears itself after a few seconds so you can simply try again instead of rebooting.
- You can now save up to 64 presets (previously limited to 16). Recordings beyond the 16th now appear in the preset list as expected.
- Fixed an audio dropout that could happen when recording into the slot you're currently playing — capturing a new sound over the one that's playing no longer stalls or glitches the output.
> Beta build for testers. First 1.1.0 preview with the new **harmonic spread** mode, on top of the Advanced Settings menu, custom scales, and modulator gain.

- **Harmonic spread mode** — a new satellite spread shape you can A/B against the original. With the spread fully open at 100 Hz, the original "expo" mode stacks octaves (100 / 200 / 400 / 800), while the new "harmonic" mode follows the harmonic series (100 / 200 / 300 / 400). At zero spread both modes sound identical, so the knob's starting point is unchanged, and the filterbank follows the new tuning. Toggle "expo" / "harmonic" on its row in the Advanced Settings menu.

- **Advanced Settings menu** — a new settings view with the list on the left and the highlighted value on the right. Enter it by holding SET at power-on, or by holding SET for 5 seconds from the main view. A single SET click saves and returns to the main view.
- **Snap pitch to a scale** — quantizes each satellite's pitch to the nearest note of a chosen scale. The default "chromatic" is a no-op and sounds exactly like v1.0.x.

- **Custom scales from SD** — add a `/scales.txt` file to the Daisy SD card to define up to 16 of your own scales, one per line as `name = 0,2,4,5,7,9,11` (semitone offsets 0–11). Lines starting with `#` and blank lines are ignored. Without the file, built-in scales are used: chromatic, octaves, fifths, major, minor, pentatonic.
- **Modulator gain** — a 0.00× – 2.00× level for the modulator (default 1.00×), affecting both the audio and the on-screen spectrum, adjustable with the touchwheel on its row.
- Advanced Settings (scale, spread mode, modulator gain) are saved on the device and restored automatically after each power cycle.
- Scale names sync to the menu, so you see the real names from your `/scales.txt` (or the built-ins) instead of plain numbers.
- The hold-SET "save" prompt now only appears in the Datum Manager, so it no longer pops up while you're holding SET to open Advanced Settings.
- Advanced Settings now reliably reach the engine and take effect as you change them.
- Settings entered by holding SET at power-on are no longer overwritten during start-up.
- This build updates both the ESP32 and the Daisy firmware — flash the full release bundle so both stay in sync.
- The harmonic mode's filter tracking is still being tuned — please A/B the two modes by ear and let us know which feels better.
### v1.0.6 - Slots, datum preview & screensaver fixes
- Slot navigation restores previously-saved slot data and prefetches the next slot
- Orbit-speed visualization auto-dismisses after its timeout
- Internal oscillator output level raised to full DAC scale
- microSD `.esp` firmware install no longer loops: the file is deleted after a successful install so it applies exactly once
- Screensaver uses now 5-minute idle timeout and is reset/woken by physical UI input
ORBIT long-press resets orbit multipliers to 1.0×; SAT long-press resets orbit offsets and triggers an orbit phase reset.
### v1.0.6b4 - Pot-touch screensaver wake (internal test build)
Carries forward everything in v1.0.6b3. Restores pot-driven screensaver control that was lost when v1.0.6b2 removed CV-driven wake wholesale — without bringing back the CV problem b2 fixed.

- Turning the KEY, SPREAD, or LENS pot now resets the screensaver idle timer and wakes the screen from the screensaver again, matching buttons / encoder / touchwheel. Pot motion is detected from the pot-only ADC on the Daisy, independent of CV, so a deliberate knob turn defers/exits the screensaver while patched CV/LFO signals still correctly let it sleep (the b2 fix is preserved). Per-channel UI animations are unchanged.

- The Daisy streams a pot-activity flag in `DaisyParams` (now 7 bytes), derived from a pot-only motion threshold. The ESP32 applies it on the UI core via an atomic rather than mutating view state from the UART task. The struct size change requires flashing both MCUs together.
### v1.0.6b3 - Firmware update flow cleanup (internal test build)
Carries forward everything in v1.0.6b2. De-confuses the two firmware-update paths: version numbers are now informational (logged) rather than a gate on whether firmware installs.

- microSD `.esp` install (the official end-user path) no longer version-gates: any `.esp` found on the SD card is installed, then deleted on success so it applies exactly once and never loops (delete-after-install, #78). Version is logged, not compared.

- ESP32 data-section OTA (the dev path — a Daisy binary placed in the ESP32 LittleFS) no longer requires a higher version number to flash. The bundled firmware is always pushed to the Daisy when present and deleted from LittleFS after a successful transfer; that delete replaces the old version check as the re-flash loop guard.

- The Daisy firmware embedded in the ESP32 data partition is now a single, version-independent file (`eisei.bin`) instead of an accumulating `eisei-X.Y.Z.bin` per build. Distributed release artifacts keep their versioned names.
- Build script and CI release pipeline emit `data/eisei.bin`; the local build removes stale versioned copies so they don't pile up in the LittleFS image.
- Removed the unused force-update (WARP-held-at-boot) code path from the ESP32 OTA sender.
### v1.0.6b2 - Screensaver fix on top of b1 (internal test build)
Carries forward everything in v1.0.6b1; adds a single screensaver fix.

- Screensaver now actually triggers when CV/LFO signals are patched. CV motion no longer defers or exits the screensaver: the six DaisyParams channels are pot+CV summed (KEY/SPREAD/LENS) or CV-only (TIME/ORBIT/WARP), so live CV kept the idle timer permanently reset and the screen never slept. The screensaver is now reset/exited solely by physical UI input (buttons, encoder, wheel). Supersedes the CV/pot-motion wake added in v1.0.2b1 (#66) and reworked through v1.0.4b5. Per-channel UI animations are unchanged.
### v1.0.6b1 - Unified bugfix beta: slots + orbits + datum preview (internal test build)

Consolidates the two parallel test lines — v1.0.4b5 (FW update / reset gestures / CV-wake / slot fixes) and v1.0.5b1 (datum preview responsiveness) — into a single build, plus the slot-restore and SD-contention work that landed after each was cut. Carries forward every fix from v1.0.2b1 through v1.0.5b1.
- Slot navigation now restores previously-saved slot data and prefetches the next slot, so scrolling between slots no longer surfaces stale/empty datum or stutters on selection.

- Saving works after a UI-driven preset load: the SavePrompt is now armed when a slot is populated from the preset list (not just from a fresh recording), so long-press SET opens the save dialog instead of being silently refused (#67).
- Slot reload drops the stale pool entry first, fixing silent load failures where a reloaded slot kept serving the previously-cached datum.
- Orbits no longer snap-resync to master phase when a satellite multiplier sits at ≈1.0, removing an unwanted phase jump.
- Removed dead/legacy button gestures and gated DatumManager actions until Daisy reports ready, preventing early-boot misfires.

- Datum preview SD access is now serialized behind the slot async loader and reads one chunk per Tick, eliminating the back-to-back `f_read` contention that could stall audio/UI while a preview load was in flight.
- DatumManager stays alive while a preview load is in flight, so scrolling mid-load no longer risks a hang.
- Preview chunk size raised 4 KB → 16 KB for faster large-file previews.
- `DEBUG_BUILD`-gated SD-contention diagnostics added around the preview/loader path. Compiled out of release builds.
### v1.0.4b6 - FW update + reset gestures + CV-wake + gesture cleanup (internal test build)

- ESP32 firmware file is now deleted from the SD card after a successful OTA transfer. Previously the file persisted, so the boot-time scan re-detected it on the next reboot and re-triggered the update procedure indefinitely (#78).

- Screensaver now defers/exits on CV motion across all six DaisyParams channels (KEY, SPREAD, LENS, TIME, ORBIT, WARP). Wake threshold floored at 0.04 normalized (KEY: 3.3 Hz) so idle ADC jitter on unplugged inputs doesn't false-wake; UI animations keep their tighter per-channel thresholds.

- SELECT long-press → DatumManager now waits for the actual Daisy handshake before opening, replacing the fixed 5s post-boot cooldown. Fixes the page sometimes opening onto an empty list when the handshake took longer than 5s.

- ORBIT long-press (bulk reset of per-satellite multipliers to 1.0×) no longer snaps satellites to a common phase. The auto-resync of per-satellite phase to the master phase on `multiplier ≈ 1.0` in `Orbits::SetPhaseMultiplier` was unintended for the v1.0 firmware and has been removed; multiplier changes now only affect speed in `Process()`.
- Replaced the v1.0.3b1 hold-SET-then-tap reset gestures with plain long-press gestures, to stop colliding with other SET actions:
- **ORBIT long-press** → reset per-satellite orbit multipliers to 1.0×
- **SAT long-press** → reset orbit offsets and trigger orbit phase reset (combines what the old SET+SAT modifier did with what ORBIT long-press did)
- Removed unused button gestures that nothing referenced anymore:
- WARP held at boot no longer forces a Daisy firmware reinstall.
- SAT + WARP held no longer enters Calibration at runtime (Calibration is still reachable by holding SELECT at boot).
- SET + SELECT no longer resets the current datum.
- Backported the ESP32 `--remote --target esp32` flash path from the v1.0.5 line so `build_eisei_firmware.sh` can build locally and flash the app partition over SSH (#82).

- Advanced Settings menu — list-left / value-right layout, entered by holding SET at boot or by holding SET for 5 seconds from the main view. Long-press SET (2s) inside the menu saves and exits back to the main view.

- Modulator gain (0.00× – 2.00×, default 1.00×) — linear multiplier applied to the modulator envelope. Affects both audio output and the spectrum drawn on screen. Adjusted via the touchwheel on the highlighted row.
- Spread parameter quantization — snaps each satellite's oscillator pitch to the nearest tone of a selected musical scale. Scale 0 (chromatic) is no-op and preserves the v1.0.x sound.

- Editable scales table on SD: `/scales.txt` on the Daisy SD card lets you define up to 16 custom scales. One scale per line, format `name = N,N,N,...` with semitone offsets in `0..11`. Comments (`#`) and blank lines are allowed; malformed lines are silently skipped.
- Built-in default scales used when `/scales.txt` is absent or yields no parseable lines: chromatic, octaves, fifths, major, minor, pentatonic.
- Persistence: advanced settings are stored in ESP32 NVS and pushed to the Daisy on every session handshake.
- Scale names sync from Daisy to ESP32 on connect, so the advanced settings menu shows the real names from `/scales.txt` (or the built-in defaults), not just generic indices.
### v1.0.4b5 - FW update + reset gestures + CV-wake (internal test build)

- ESP32 firmware file is now deleted from the SD card after a successful OTA transfer. Previously the file persisted, so the boot-time scan re-detected it on the next reboot and re-triggered the update procedure indefinitely (#78).

- Screensaver now defers/exits on CV motion across all six DaisyParams channels (KEY, SPREAD, LENS, TIME, ORBIT, WARP). Wake threshold floored at 0.04 normalized (KEY: 3.3 Hz) so idle ADC jitter on unplugged inputs doesn't false-wake; UI animations keep their tighter per-channel thresholds.
- Replaced the v1.0.3b1 hold-SET-then-tap reset gestures with plain long-press gestures, to stop colliding with other SET actions:
- **ORBIT long-press** → reset per-satellite orbit multipliers to 1.0×
- **SAT long-press** → reset orbit offsets and trigger orbit phase reset (combines what the old SET+SAT modifier did with what ORBIT long-press did)
### v1.0.4b4 - FW update + reset gestures + CV-wake (internal test build)

- ESP32 firmware file is now deleted from the SD card after a successful OTA transfer. Previously the file persisted, so the boot-time scan re-detected it on the next reboot and re-triggered the update procedure indefinitely (#78).

- Screensaver now defers/exits on CV motion across all six DaisyParams channels (KEY, SPREAD, LENS, TIME, ORBIT, WARP). The check uses the same per-field thresholds that already drive UI animations (e.g. the warp pulse when a warp is the CV target), so anything moving the UI counts as activity. Previous b2/b3 attempts used wrong signals (DaisyParams with coarse threshold; LED-stream with jack-plug gating) and missed steady-state CV motion.
- Replaced the v1.0.3b1 hold-SET-then-tap reset gestures with plain long-press gestures, to stop colliding with other SET actions:
- **ORBIT long-press** → reset per-satellite orbit multipliers to 1.0×
- **SAT long-press** → reset orbit offsets and trigger orbit phase reset (combines what the old SET+SAT modifier did with what ORBIT long-press did)
### v1.0.4b3 - FW update + reset gestures + CV-wake (internal test build)

- ESP32 firmware file is now deleted from the SD card after a successful OTA transfer. Previously the file persisted, so the boot-time scan re-detected it on the next reboot and re-triggered the update procedure indefinitely (#78).

- Screensaver now exits when a CV cable is inserted or a Daisy-side pot is moved. The wake check is hooked to the UI LED stream, which is jack-gated for the orbit and input channels — so a cable insertion produces a clean transition instead of getting lost in unplugged-ADC drift (v1.0.4b2's attempt sat on DaisyParams and didn't trigger reliably).
- Replaced the v1.0.3b1 hold-SET-then-tap reset gestures with plain long-press gestures, to stop colliding with other SET actions:
- **ORBIT long-press** → reset per-satellite orbit multipliers to 1.0×
- **SAT long-press** → reset orbit offsets and trigger orbit phase reset (combines what the old SET+SAT modifier did with what ORBIT long-press did)
### v1.0.4b2 - FW update + reset gestures + CV-wake (internal test build)

- ESP32 firmware file is now deleted from the SD card after a successful OTA transfer. Previously the file persisted, so the boot-time scan re-detected it on the next reboot and re-triggered the update procedure indefinitely (#78).
- Screensaver now exits on CV/pot motion, not only on ESP32 button/encoder/wheel input. Extends the v1.0.2b1 entry-deferral fix to the active-screensaver case using the same motion thresholds.
- Replaced the v1.0.3b1 hold-SET-then-tap reset gestures with plain long-press gestures, to stop colliding with other SET actions:
- **ORBIT long-press** → reset per-satellite orbit multipliers to 1.0×
- **SAT long-press** → reset orbit offsets and trigger orbit phase reset (combines what the old SET+SAT modifier did with what ORBIT long-press did)
### v1.0.3b2 - Osc volume + UI gesture cleanup (internal test build)
Re-cut: v1.0.3b1 build failed on build-esp32 because the #74 hold-modifier
gesture used an `AbstractInputManager::GetPressDuration` method that only
existed on the Daisy concrete class. Contents are otherwise identical to b1.
- Internal oscillator output level raised to full DAC scale — addresses "low volume vs other brands" reports (#77).

- Orbit speed visualization now auto-dismisses after the timeout instead of staying pinned until the user cycles through warps. Root cause was a vestigial lens-pot/CV handler that kept resetting the view timer on every analog drift sample (#79).
- RESET TIME and RESET ORBIT redesigned as hold-modifier gestures, replacing the broken simultaneous-press combo (#74):
- RESET TIME: hold SET ≥ 1 s, then tap SATELLITE
- RESET ORBIT: hold SET ≥ 1 s, then tap ORBIT encoder
### v1.0.4b1 - Firmware update loop fix (internal test build)

- ESP32 firmware file is now deleted from the SD card after a successful OTA transfer. Previously the file persisted, so the boot-time scan re-detected it on the next reboot and re-triggered the update procedure indefinitely (#78).
### v1.0.2b1 - Screensaver + slot save (internal test build)
- Screensaver no longer spawns after 2 minutes — inactivity timeout bumped to 5 minutes (#66).
- Screensaver entry is also deferred by CV input motion and Daisy-side pot interaction, not only ESP32 buttons/wheel (#66).
- Saving a slot after loading a preset from the list now works — long-press SET opens the SavePrompt instead of being silently refused (#67).

- Restored full key_hz routing (pot + CV) to the filterbank. High CV now saturates filterbank pitch at 440 Hz instead of being silently dropped, while oscillators continue to track. The original SVF instability is already neutralised upstream by the FilterBank LUT clamp work.
- Adaptive SDRAM datum cache: per-datum allocation scales 4× and loads on demand, reducing static SDRAM pressure.
- Removed dead `key_pot_hz` field and its plumbing through `AudioControlSnapshot`, `EngineParams`, and `AudioProcessor`.
### v1.0.1b2 - Filterbank CV + SDRAM cache (internal test build)

- Restored full key_hz routing (pot + CV) to the filterbank. High CV now saturates filterbank pitch at 440 Hz instead of being silently dropped, while oscillators continue to track. The original SVF instability is already neutralised upstream by the FilterBank LUT clamp work.
- Adaptive SDRAM datum cache: per-datum allocation scales 4× and loads on demand, reducing static SDRAM pressure.
- Removed dead `key_pot_hz` field and its plumbing through `AudioControlSnapshot`, `EngineParams`, and `AudioProcessor`.
### v1.0.1b1 - Filterbank CV Tracking Fix (internal test build)

- Restored full key_hz routing (pot + CV) to the filterbank. High CV now saturates filterbank pitch at 440 Hz instead of being silently dropped, while oscillators continue to track. The original SVF instability is already neutralised upstream by the FilterBank LUT clamp work.
- Removed dead `key_pot_hz` field and its plumbing through `AudioControlSnapshot`, `EngineParams`, and `AudioProcessor`.
- Datum Manager UI with smooth list scrolling, orbit playhead, inactivity timeout, and loaded-datum jump
- 8 built-in algorithmic datum presets (VCA, Mirror, Chirp, Binary, Harmonic, etc.) filling all default slots
- Datum preview system with real LUT preset loading, interpolation, and 30Hz refresh
- Health diagnostics system with audio loopback test tone for calibration jig
- OTA verbose error info — error reason displayed on OTA progress screen
- Screen centering adjustment integrated into Calibration scene with NVS persistence
- Scanner CV remapped: reversed positive range, permutation-chained negative range
- Lens pot curve improved for better control feel
- LED brightness halved globally; REC input LED turns off when nothing connected
- Audio LED fix for accurate level display
- Comprehensive unit test suite (57+ tests) covering DatumManager, persistence, protocol dispatch
- Code quality sweep: extracted CommService, CallbackHandlers, and engine logic from main.cpp; replaced volatile with std::atomic
- Removed `Serial.setDebugOutput` and unused temporary variables from production build
- SD card firmware cleanup on boot to prevent bootloader auto-flash revert
- Fixed SRAM overflow on Daisy (106.92% to 79.42%) by moving LUT arrays to SDRAM
- Fixed satellite position wiggle when orbit rate is zero
- Fixed DatumManager orbit playhead draw order and visibility
- Fixed encoder bounce and missed detents with halfQuad accumulator
- Fixed datum list-request race condition with retry on dropped initial send
- Fixed PWM range limiting to avoid silence at extremes
- Fixed C_List culling, centering, and selection offset
- Fixed boot timing and audio loopback probe detection
- Fixed CI build (octal version constant, ESP32 font path, ARM GCC pinning)
- screen alignment test scene for panel assembly with concentric circles, crosshair, and corner markers (shows on first boot or REC held at power-on)
- centralized screen center constants (SCREEN_CENTER_X/Y) replacing 40+ hardcoded values across enjin, enjin2, UI components, and scene files
- bumped GitHub Actions to Node.js 24 compatible versions
- resolved all compiler warnings in enjin/enjin2 libraries (-Wshadow, -Wsign-conversion, 0xFFFF overflow)
### v0.5.0 - LittleFS Data Partition in Release Pipeline
- release pipeline now builds and publishes a LittleFS image containing the Daisy firmware for ESP32 data partition flashing
- Lowered Daisy Uart DMA interrupt priority to prevent pops in audio processing
- improved uart communication setup for less errors
- fixed debug and release builds scripts
### v0.3.0 - Audio Performance & Filter Bank Fixes
- band solo feature added to Spectra processor (allows soloing individual bands 0-19 or 'off' via serial command)
- removed printf from audio processing path
- replaced powf(10.f, x) with pow10f(x) for ~90% speedup
- optimized InterpolateFrames to blur result once instead of 2-4 input frames
- debugging outputs now only added on debug builds
- fixed filter bank silent low bands by regenerating coefficients for 48kHz sample rate (was incorrectly using 96kHz coefficients, causing first 5 bands to be at half frequency)
- fixed POT_KEY mapping to use exponential scaling for A1 at noon (now outputs 55Hz instead of ~69Hz, ensuring equal octaves per rotation)
- completed the save prompt ui scenes
- added new save prompt ui elements
- save prompt UI now able to work for more than 1 time per session :)
### v0.2.4 - New Save Prompt UI and Warp UI
- new save prompt UI with improved animation and feedback
- new warp UI with improved animation and feedback
### v0.2.3f - Scanner and reset functionality restored
- switched from I2C to UART communication protocol between ESP32 and Daisy
- new lens algorithm with non-centered support and improved range
- lensed datum view when satellite is selected
- optional FPS counter for screen UI
- blur warp effect replacing feedback processor
- better communication protocol structure for Daisy side
- better pot ranges for key input
- lowered pot deadband and button debouncing for better responsiveness
- restored get commands for serial command manager
- fixed communication initialization (working rx and tx)
- fixed UART DMA buffer issues with non-cached buffer and robust error recovery
- code cleanup (removed repeated import, protocol-test directory)
- serial output via both USB ports
- cv input 4 now used for audio scanner control
- serial output via external USB port
- get commands in serial command manager for parameter queries
- new serial commands for extended control
- calibration manager system for improved calibration workflow
- debug and release firmware builds for Daisy platform
- enhanced calibration process with better user feedback
- flash script now waits for device connection
- serial command helpers for improved command handling
- fixed exclusive button states to prevent conflicts
### v0.2.0 - Slot Management & Remote Control Enhancements
- slot management system with assignment and loading
- async slot loading to prevent system hangs
- serial remote control interface for parameter control
- status UI with animated save/load feedback
- datum preview shows raw data instead of post-lens
- improved slot loading for all slots during initialization
- memory management with static SDRAM buffers
- fixed datum view to show raw data instead of post-lens
- resolved slot mapping issues during initialization
- reverted key tracking changes that caused issues with key cv in
- Lowered message rates, increased timeouts and retries.
- Removed the now obsolete Record message.
- Hardened I2C communication with less overhead and better error handling
### v0.1.8b - Orbit UI Refinement & Engine Simplification
- Redesigned Orbit UI with enhanced visual feedback and improved user experience
- Added new orbit display assets including frequency, multiplier, position, and target indicators
- Streamlined firmware architecture with legacy engine focus for improved stability
- Updated number font rendering for better readability in orbit displays
- Enhanced orbit UI layout with more intuitive visual elements
- Simplified filter bank coefficients management by focusing on legacy static tables
- Reduced code complexity in datum engine implementations
- Fixed font naming consistency in number rendering system
- Resolved coefficient table management issues by simplifying to legacy-only approach

- Introduced a selectable datum engine pipeline with runtime-switchable legacy static coefficients; toggle via the USB `engine mode <current|legacy>` command or the `EISEI_DATUM_ENGINE_MODE` environment variable to compare behaviors without reflashing.
- Added a 45-second "Naptime" screensaver scene with 1-bit animation, automatic contrast dimming after 25 seconds, and instant wake on any control input to protect the display.
- Applied Mutable Instruments-style encoder acceleration (up to 24x) so orbit and parameter sweeps keep up with fast gestures while preserving fine control.
- Orbit UI now clamps multipliers to +/-16x, uses 0.25 steps, and keeps displays in sync when selecting satellites (resolves GitHub issues #40 and #28).
- Converted Warp, PopUp, and feedback assets plus the sprite pipeline to 1-bit monochrome, reducing memory pressure and matching hardware vs Rack output.
- Extended USB serial diagnostics with `engine` and `engine-slots` commands plus richer logging for orbit phases, warp states, and slot contents.
- Hardened hardware bring-up: enabled Daisy Seed USART1 pull-ups for I²C stability, lowered the OLED SPI rate, and tidied ESP32 build steps.
- Stabilized the filter bank under extreme CV inputs by regenerating coefficients safely and adding legacy static tables.
- Stopped the REC input from muting oscillator D and restored touchwheel lock persistence.
- Fixed screensaver animation playback, odd-width sprite stride errors, and satellite trail defaults that left ghost artifacts.
- Corrected negative orbit value clamping so encoder-driven ranges apply across presets.
- Resolved duplicate ESP32 flash builds and missing I²C includes that previously blocked cross-target builds.
- clamp and reproject start/end settings via Orbits::UpdateRange so Daisy, VCV, and preset restores keep responding after each edit
- fix warp asset lookups (tanh vs shape), rename the warp buffers accordingly, and bound oscillator amplitude/Orbit control to the 110 Hz spec
- expand infrastructure: broaden .gitignore, stop Spectra from force-wrapping frequencies, pull in SharedData’s <string>, and add the build/dual-serial helper scripts
### v0.1.5 - Rec Fix, warp ui, popup ui
- warp ui now react to cv
- popup ui now available for notifications and instructions
- fixed rec button not working (needs hardware fix)
- Warp UI now includes a slider that visually represents the warp amount.
- Warp icon updates dynamically based on the selected warp type.
